Curriculum Vitae

Bo Zhao

Professor, Department of Geography · University of Washington
Director, Humanistic GIS Laboratory

Smith Hall 412E · zhaobo@uw.edu · hgis.uw.edu

Download CV (PDF) Last updated November 17, 2025

Statement

I identify myself as a humanistic scholar in geographic information and technology, committed to cultivating spatial perspectives that deepen our understanding of ourselves and the communities we inhabit — especially amid the grand challenges of our time. My work began with digital mapping projects in response to the Wenchuan Earthquake, helping the public visualize the disaster's spatial impact. Later, I developed a web-based platform leveraging Volunteered Geographic Information (VGI) to support street beggar children in reuniting with their families. These early experiences shaped my belief that geographic information is not only a technical instrument but also a moral and affective medium — capable of care, connection, and collective memory.

Over the years, I have extended this vision across diverse sociotechnical and cultural contexts. I examine how geospatial technologies can support the land claims of Sioux tribes, amplify the struggles and resilience of business owners of color affected by misinformation and pandemic disruption, and help reconstruct the long-silenced urban histories of LGBTQ+ communities. At the same time, I interrogate the broader societal consequences of emerging geospatial technologies. In an era often dominated by techno-optimism and algorithmic abstraction, I engage critically with phenomena such as deepfake geography, the collapse of GeoAI, and the reproduction of societal bias in geospatial big data.

For me, GIS encodes values, shapes visibility and invisibility, and reflects the uneven contours of power and privilege. I approach mapping as a practice of ethical reflection and social imagination — one that resists structural bias, attends to vulnerability, and aspires toward more inclusive human futures. Through research, teaching, and public scholarship, I continue to explore how geospatial technologies can serve not only as tools for representation, but also as instruments for empathy, justice, and human understanding.

Academic Positions

University of Washington

2025—
Professor, Department of Geography. Affiliations: eScience Institute, China Studies Program, Center for Studies in Demography & Ecology, Center for Informed Public, and Urban Design & Planning Program.
2021—25
Associate Professor, Department of Geography
2019—21
Assistant Professor, Department of Geography

Oregon State University

2016—19
Assistant Professor, College of Earth, Ocean, and Atmospheric Sciences

Harvard University

2016—17
Non-residential Fellow, J.F. Kennedy School of Government
2015—16
Rajawali Fellow, J.F. Kennedy School of Government

Education

2012—15
Ph.D. in Geography, Ohio State University
2006—08
M.S. in Cartography and GIScience, Nanjing University, China
2002—06
B.S. Honors in GIScience, Nanjing University, China

Fields of Specialization

  • Humanistic GIS, social implications of emerging GIS technologies.
  • Fake Geographies, deepfake geography and location spoofing.
  • Geospatial Artificial Intelligence (GeoAI) and Spatial Data Science.

Selected Grants

  • 2020—24. NSF Human-Environment and Geographical Sciences (HEGS): Examining Shifting Geographies of Historically Underrepresented Groups (1951072). $395,839. Co-PI.
  • 2019—21. NSF Coastlines and People (CoPe) EAGER: Coastal Hazard Planning in Time (1940024). $297,288. Co-PI.
  • 2024. UW Data Science Minor Grant: GeoAI for Social Sciences and Humanities. $12,800. PI.
  • 2023. Critical and Creative (Un)Mapping of the Capitol Hill Organized Protest, UW Humanities Data Science Summer Institute. ~$22,000. Co-PI.
  • 2022. Samsung/Joule: Machine Learning to Detect Deepfaked Satellite Imagery. $64,970. PI.
  • 2022. Population Health Initiative: Better Methods to Enumerate Individuals Experiencing Homelessness. $106,822. Co-PI.
  • 2022. UW Data Science Minor Grant: Web Programming for GIS Applications. $20,000. PI.
  • 2021—22. Urban@UW Research Spark: Improving Enumeration of Homelessness Using Cell Phone Location Data. $19,299. Co-PI.
  • 2020—21. NIH NHLBI PETAL Network (CORAL Study, 3U01HL123009-06S2). $69,173. Site PI.
  • 2020. Population Health Initiative: COVID-19 Smart Dashboard for Risk to Washington Workers. $19,537. PI.
  • 2020. UW Data Science Minor Grant: Redeveloping GEOG 458. $20,000. PI.
  • 2018—20. TWCA, Geovisual Analytic Platform. $15,000. PI.
  • 2018—19. Clean Water Service: Fanno Creek Watershed. $50,000. PI.
  • 2018—19. National Geographic. The Global Refugee Atlas (NGS-51699S-18). $68,383. PI.
  • 2017—18. Clean Water Service. Map-based Storytelling of Reuse Water. $50,000. PI.
  • 2016—17. Learning Innovation Grant: VR for Geovisualization. $9,857. PI.
  • 2015—16. Rajawali Fellowship, Harvard Kennedy School. $50,000.

Publications — In Preparation

  • 2026 Lin, Y. & B. Zhao. GeoAI Embracing Place? The Pitfalls of Earth Embeddings and Digital Placelessness. Annals of the American Association of Geographers.
  • 2025 (under review) Zhao B. Fake Geographies. MIT Press. Cambridge, MA.
  • 2024 Knopp, L., B. Zhao, & M. Brown. Shifting Spatialities of LGBTQ Life. Annals of the American Association of Geographers.

Publications — Journal Articles

  • 2026 Zhao B., Y. Lin. GeoAI collapse? Ethical implications of synthetic geospatial data use. International Journal of Geographical Information Science.
  • 2026 Luo, C., P. Zhao, B. Wang, S. Feng, Y., & L. Meng. Ethical Spatial Analysis: Addressing Endogenous Bias Through Visual Analytics. Journal of Geovisualization and Spatial Analysis, 10(1), 4.
  • 2026 Huang, T., X. Ye, T. Yigitcanlar, B. Xu, G. Newman, B. Zhao, … & D. Wang. Artificial intelligence in urban design: A systematic review. Cities, 169, 106527.
  • 2025 Hong, R., B. Zhao, & S. Zhou. Root metaphors in cartography: Bridging critical and quantitative approaches. Transactions of the Institute of British Geographers, e70035.
  • 2025 Ma, X., Zheng, G., Xu, C., Moskal, L. M., Gong, P., Guo, Q., … & B. Zhao, Zhou, Y. A global product of 150-m urban building height based on spaceborne lidar. Scientific Data, 11(1), 1387.
  • 2025 Yang, T., B. Zhao, S. Gao, & W. Dong. Neural representation of geoinformation in the human brain. Annals of GIS, 1—16.
  • 2025 Frazier, A., T. Nelson, P. Kedron, E. Shook, S. Dodge, … B. Zhao, W. Xu. GIScience Education in an Age of Disruptions. Transactions in GIS, 29(2), e70048.
  • 2025 Lin, Y. & B. Zhao. Post-human cartography? Rethinking artificial intelligence, cartographic practices, and reflexivity. Annals of the American Association of Geographers, 115(3), 499—512.
  • 2024 Dodge, S., A. Frazier, M. Goodchild, P. Kedron, … B. Zhao, …, J. Wilson. A Research Agenda for GIScience and Spatial Data Science Amid Disruptions to Science. International Journal of Geographical Information Science, 39(1), 1—24.
  • 2024 Zhao B., J. Feng, Y. Sun, X. Chang, & S. Shaw. Neural Sensing: Towards a new approach to understanding emotional responses to place. Transactions in GIS.
  • 2024 Zhao, B. & L. Zhang. Understanding the Spatial Narratives of Puzzle Films Using Cognitive Maps. Professional Geographer, 76(4): 450—457.
  • 2024 Lin, B., L. Zou, B. Zhao, X. Huang, H. Cai, M. Yang, & B. Zhou. Sensing the Pulse of the Pandemic. Cartography and Geographic Information Science, 51(3): 366—384.
  • 2024 Peng, L. & B. Zhao. Navigating the Ethical Landscape Behind ChatGPT. Big Data & Society, 11(1).
  • 2024 Feng, J., B. Zhao, & S. Shaw. The Study of Emotion in GIScience: Status and Prospects. Cartography and Geographic Information Science.
  • 2024 Zhao, B. Emplacing the disposition towards automation. Dialogue in Human Geography, 14(1): 85—88.
  • 2024 Zhao, B. Reorient GIScience for a Data-intensive Society. Dialogue in Human Geography, 14(2): 327—331.
  • 2023 Yang, Y., Chen, X., Gao, S., Li, Z., Zhang, Z., & Zhao, B. Embracing geospatial analytical technologies in tourism studies. Information Technology & Tourism, 25(2): 137—150.
  • 2023 Huang, X., X. Bao, Z. Li, S. Zhang, B. Zhao. Black businesses matter: A longitudinal study of black-owned restaurants in the COVID-19 Pandemic. Annals of the American Association of Geographers, 113(1): 189—205.
  • 2022 Yu, Y., B. Dylan, B. Zhao. Digital geographies of the bug: A case study of China's contact tracing systems. GeoForum, 137: 94—104.
  • 2022 Bennett, M. M., Van Den Hoek, J., B. Zhao & Prishchepov, A. V. Improving Satellite Monitoring of Armed Conflicts. Earth's Future, 10(90): 1—6.
  • 2022 Zhao, B. Humanistic GIS: Towards a Research Agenda. Annals of the American Association of Geographers, 112(6): 1576—1592.
  • 2021 Zhao, B., S. Zhang, C. Xu, Y. Sun, and C. Deng. Deep fake geography? When geospatial data encounter artificial intelligence. Cartography and Geographic Information Science, 48(4): 338—352.
  • 2021 Zhang, S., B. Zhao, Y. Tian, and S. Chen. Stand with #StandingRock: Envisioning an epistemological shift in understanding geospatial big data in the "post-truth" era. Annals of the American Association of Geographers, 111(4): 1025—1045.
  • 2020 Zhao, B., & X. Huang. Encrypted monument: The birth of crypto place on the blockchain. GeoForum, 116: 149—152.
  • 2019 Zhao, B., X. Huang, and D.Z. Sui. Place spoofing: A case study of the xenophilic copycat community in Beijing, China. Professional Geographer, 71(2): 265—277.
  • 2019 Zhao, B. & S. Zhang. Rethinking spatial data quality: Pokémon Go as a case study of location spoofing. Professional Geographer, 70(4): 1—13.
  • 2017 Zhao, B. & D. Z. Sui. True lies in geospatial big data: detecting location spoofing in social media. Annals of GIS, 23(1): 1—14.
  • 2017 Zhao, B., D.Z. Sui & Z. Li. Visualizing the gay community in Beijing with location-based social media. Environment and Planning A, 49(5): 977—79.

Conference proceedings, Chinese-language publications, and earlier articles are listed in the downloadable CV.

Public Scholarship — Software & Tools

  • 2023. Archiving the Capitol Hill Organized Protest. Humanistic GIS Laboratory. doi:10.6069/4KHS-3W59. With Koopman, D., Jung, J.-K., Sun, H., & Anderson, C.
  • 2022. Queer Space in the United States from 2000 to 2019. Web map of Damron Men's Travel Guide venues. doi:10.6069/WT82-TH75. With Zhang, Q., Xia, Y., Huang, J., & Peng, L.
  • 2020. Novel coronavirus infection map. hgis.uw.edu/virus. With Fengyu Xu, Lola Kang, Joshua Ji, Jou Ho, and Steven Bao.
  • 2018. Geovisual analytical platform for Google Earth Engine. hgis.uw.edu/geeviz. Supported by Google Cloud Platform. With Hannah Friedrich.
  • 2018. Vulnerability of shellfish stakeholders to ocean acidification, PNW. hgis.uw.edu/pnw. NOAA NA17OAR0170165.
  • 2017. Sea Level Rise Induced Migration Project. hgis.uw.edu/slr. Grand Prize, Ecological Visualization Contest. NSF-DBI-1062566.
  • 2016. Storymap.js. A JavaScript library for telling a story with web maps. github.com/jakobzhao/storymap.
